cells.notes: Exact-name recall is strong: 15/15 sampled records rank top-1 for their exact name (MYKOBO, Clickspesa, Spiko, QCAD, GetBlock, xlm.sh, Webacy, Refractor, Bondhive, Lumenswap, Arka.fund, Bloccpay, XBid AI, Fewticket, Simbolik, plus Helix/Keybase/WarpDrive across statuses), and all 4 known entities (Freighter, Soroswap, Blend, StellarExpert) are top-1. Space-split aliases (bond hive, lumen swap, blocc pay, warp drive, few ticket, soro swap) all resolve top-1. SYSTEMIC: the semantic lane only SUPPLEMENTS queries with >=1 keyword hit — zero-keyword-hit queries (misspellings mikobo/spico/simbolic, slug forms dcent/xlmsh) return total:0 with no vector fallback; misspelling zeros were graded lenient-OK (not record-data-implied) but the punctuation/slug class was filed since the slug IS record data. Both filed dupe pairs lack canonicalSlug; note scripts/detect-duplicate-projects.ts exists untracked in the working tree — the live corpus still serves at least these 2 pairs. Tolerances respected: no WRONG-TOP filed for equal-validity ties (e.g. 'canadian dollar stablecoin' → stablecorp (QCAD issuer) over qcad is fine).

Coverage: all 19 types probed with natural browse queries (Bridge skipped per instructions); 16/28 probes clean — dex, lending protocols, payments, fiat-on-ramp-anchor, stablecoin, wallet, nft, gaming, ai agents, security audit, rpc provider, indexer, rwa, real-world-assets, faucet-recall, cdp all show type-matching records dominating top-10 in prominence-ish order. SYSTEMIC PATTERN 1: type membership does NOT drive inclusion — the sls-018/019 'structured truth must drive inclusion' class at the type level. A record is only retrievable by its type name if its prose happens to contain that word (proven for Social Impact 3/15, Education 13/36, SDK 63/141, Analytics 22/58; RWA 24-of-85 on 'real world assets' same shape but top-10 unharmed). Fix candidate: index types[] into the keyword match / add type-name synonym expansion (dex↔decentralized exchange). SYSTEMIC PATTERN 2: semantic-lane hits serialize types=[] and prominence=null for records that have both (proven twice, different records) — misleading for agent consumers. SYSTEMIC PATTERN 3: name-variant duplicate rows (stellar-expert/stellarexpert, coins-ph/coinsph, cash-abroad/cashabroad) co-surface in single result sets — scripts/detect-duplicate-projects.ts presumably targets this; these three pairs are live now. Live-first ordering held everywhere checked (Inactive OrbitCDP below Live on q=cdp; Pre-Release Zenex above Live DEXs only inside the already-filed decentralized-exchange finding; Development raum-network above Live ties on q=dex not filed — tie-order taste). Prominence ordering is non-monotonic within same-type results (Kale prom=90 at rank 3 under prom-80s on q=gaming; python-sdk 85 above soroban-rust-sdk 96 on q=sdk) — some other score component dominates; not filed since all candidates equally on-topic, but worth a look if ordering complaints recur.

Coverage: 23 live search probes + 2 record-level integrity checks; ground truth built by paging all 892 category-reachable projects into a local table (fields only, read-only). OK lanes worth crediting: country keyword queries over coverage-carrying anchors are healthy (kenya→honey-coin #1, tanzania→clickspesa #2 + tzs via semantic, argentina→bitso/anclap/ping, philippines→coins-ph #1, mexico anchor→bitso/cash-abroad); ticker queries (KES, NGNC) hit #1; supportedNetworks non-bridge carriers retrievable (xrpl wallet → lobstr #1, ultra-stellar #4); scfAwarded=true composes correctly with q (gaming top-8 = 8/8 prose-matching GT winners); euro anchor → mykobo #1. Systemic root causes behind most failures: (1) keyword search matches name+prose only — types, coverage.seps, coverage.currencies never drive inclusion (same class as the sls-018/019 structured-recall fix, unfixed for these fields); (2) 59 records have shortDescription=null, including 28/32 Asset tickers, making the whole currency-name lane invisible except substring luck; (3) semantic augmentation fires inconsistently (on for kenya/tanzania/euro-anchor, off for kenyan-shilling/argentine-peso despite total=1); (4) matchMode=majority substring inflation (peruvian sol → 113 results via 'sol'). Avoided bridge/ramp/Etherfuse anchoring per instructions — supportedNetworks tested via the only non-bridge carriers (lobstr/ultra-stellar).

Positives (OK evidence): status is served on every search row; orbitcdp's lifecycle block is exemplary honesty ({wasLive:true, note: shut down 2026, pivoted to Zenex} — successor pointer included); direct name/status queries work ('orbitcdp status' top-1, 'is orbitcdp still active' top-2 via semantic); crowded-query Live ordering holds (q=wallet top-10 all Live); category browse (no q) works and ranks prominent Live projects first; clusters contain none of the 9 non-Live samples; analyze rejects unknown dimension with an honest validDimensions list; absent projects (communidao/lumosdao/instantdao/enerdao/fxdao — the previously-flagged defunct DAOs appear to have been REMOVED from the directory rather than mislabeled) return honest 0-result advisories; moneygram's 'launched' prose is dated (June 2026), not undated-recency. Coverage caveats: only 9/921 projects are non-Live (0.98%) — lifecycle coverage itself is almost certainly under-populated (per known defunct-detection gap), but no specific status mislabel was provable from record-own data in this run since the known-defunct names are now absent entirely. Systemic pattern: retrieval failures cluster on QUERY-SHAPE (hyphenated slugs, name+filler-word questions, intent-vs-keyword temporal queries) rather than on the data, and the semantic fallback fires inconsistently (rescued orbitcdp phrasing, not keybase phrasing).

Coverage: 19 distinct consumer intents probed across yield, remittances (PH/MX/Kenya), real estate, savings groups, trading, loans, stocks, gold, charity, bills, dollar savings, wallets, bill-splitting, NFTs. Strong areas: geo remittance corridors for Mexico/Kenya (felix-pago, honey-coin #1 with on-point records), lending (blend/slender top-2 for 'get a loan'), savings circles (vaquita/sava), NFT art, bill-splitting (splito found via semantic). All returned records were status=Live — no stale/Inactive records surfaced in any consumer top-8, and no JUNK-ROWs. Systemic pattern #1: the matchMode fallback ladder (strict→majority→loose-1) preserves result volume but discards the intent-bearing RARE token — common verbs (buy/get/send/save) dominate ranking while the rare noun (gold, stocks, philippines) either ranks 10+ or drops out entirely; scoring should weight rare-token hits (IDF-style) above common-token hits. Systemic pattern #2: no stemming (donate/donations, save/savings, charity/charities) causes hard recall misses on the most natural consumer phrasings. Systemic pattern #3: the semantic lane does not rescue geo-specific recall (coins-ph missed even with sem=true). Minor: keyword-lane confidence score is a uniform 0.97 across nearly all keyword hits — non-discriminative for consumers of the API. Gaming projects (warmancer/beamable) intrude at #3-4 on 'earn yield' via play-to-EARN wording but blend/aquarius/slender still hold 3 of top-5, graded OK.

Systemic root cause behind all WRONG-TOP/RECALL-MISS rows: superlative tokens are literal AND-keywords scored by match-count. Two regimes: (1) tokens rare in prose ('biggest','best') are effectively no-ops — top-10 identical to the plain noun, prominence-ordered, graceful (biggest dex, best oracle, best anchor, best/biggest lending protocol, top rpc provider, most popular nft marketplace, soroswap vs phoenix all OK); (2) tokens common in marketing prose ('most','used','popular','active','largest','volume','tvl') let prose-coincidence rows score 2-3 and — critically — the confidence layer stamps them 0.92-0.97/high (relevance=1) while the true category leaders drop to 0.6-0.65/medium or out of the set entirely. The confidence signal is INVERTED exactly on superlative queries, which is the most agent-misleading artifact in this cell: an agent taking top-1+high-confidence as 'the most used wallet' gets Sava. Semantic fallback only fires on 0 keyword hits, so tiny bad keyword sets (largest dex by volume, total=2) serve as-is. On the cell's data question: NO tvl/usage/volume field exists anywhere in the corpus (projects carry prominence/scfTotalAwardedUSD/repo stars; /api/leaderboard is GitHub-activity + Electric Capital dev counts and honestly labels sort=activity in filters; /api/analyze dims = all/hackathons/categories/funding) — superlatives are unanswerable-by-data and no meta note says so. Suggested fix shape: strip/stopword superlative tokens before matching (making regime 2 behave like regime 1, i.e. prominence-ordered nouns), and consider a meta note that ordering=relevance+prominence, not size/usage. Marginal-but-tolerated: 'top wallets' interleaves Warmancer(Gaming)/DFNS/Beamable at #3-5 above Freighter (#7) — same class, leaders still top-2 so counted OK; 'most secure wallet' counted OK since #1 SocketFi is a wallet by prose (see DATA-WRONG) and Ledger/Trezor are top-4. Not re-audited per no-anchoring rule: bridges/ramps/Etherfuse. Baselines run: dex, wallet, oracle, stablecoin, lending, blend.

Coverage: 42 probes, 11 verticals; 11 fully-OK probes (wallet, sdk, wallet+sdk, webauthn, kyc, mobile, react-native, wallet&language=Rust filter, disbursement, litemint, payments). Systemic patterns: (1) Ranking is keyword-token-score-first (name token=5 > desc token=3) with NO Stellar-relevance factor, and the corpus contains org-swept non-Stellar repos (small-org gate) — so empty-description dead other-chain repos beat codeVerified Stellar repos on nearly every niche vertical (nft, governance, testing, game, flutter, indexer, audit). Same class as the documented repo-search relevance defect, reproduced on NEW verticals. (2) q does not search the owner field and is unstemmed (fuzzing/fuzzer, gaming/game). (3) Multi-word q uses OR semantics — q=smart+wallet total=250 vs q=wallet total=93 — inflating totals though top-K stays usable. (4) Hackathon judgeScore-inflated repoScores (69-85) let Feb/Apr-2026 prototypes crowd q=payments/q=game tops; not filed for payments since anchor-platform correctly leads and x402 rows are on-topic, but it suppresses diversity. (5) Not filed under ground-truth rules but worth a synonyms pass: stellar-disbursement-platform is unreachable via q=payments because its own name/desc/topics lack the word. (6) q=governance served score=0 rows at ranks 2-3 (hidden pin/boost?) — on-topic so not filed, but the scoring contract looks violated. (7) Dupe scan covered only 300 of 546 q=stellar rows; the case-variant dupe class likely has more instances corpus-wide.

Fair-grading summary: the identifier-form fix DOES generalize beyond the two original test symbols — 38/47 probes OK across 16 distinct symbol-carrying repos and topics (dex, flight insurance, ZK privacy, smart accounts, MCP tooling, scaffolding, DeFi vaults, DID, oracles, streaming payments). Exact snake_case, exact camelCase/CamelCase, and spaced natural forms of pure-alpha symbols all retrieve the carrier at rank 0-1; multi-carrier symbol SignerKey surfaces both carriers top-2. The ONE systemic residual gap: identifier sub-tokens containing digits are only searchable when underscore-delimited (bn256_modulus OK spaced) but NOT when CamelCase-embedded — groth16, secp256r1, ed25519, scval all fail as standalone/natural queries (q=groth16 and q=secp256r1 return total=0 corpus-wide), likely a letter/digit boundary split on the doc side with no matching split (or no concatenation-preserving token) on the query side. Since crypto-algorithm names (ed25519, secp256r1, groth16, bn254/bls12-381 class) are exactly what agent users ask for, this class is worth fixing once centrally. Secondary tendency (borderline, mostly within tolerance): when a symbol decomposes into ubiquitous tokens, authority-ranked generic-token matches can outrank the exact-symbol carrier (AdminOraclePage carrier ranked 5th behind 4 oracle repos — within top-5, counted OK; DidStellarRegistry ranked 6th — filed as WRONG-TOP). Not probed: symbols on repos outside the topic samples used (sampling was via q=soroban/wallet/defi/payments/oracle/nft/dex/lending/amm/token, 18 symbol-bearing repos found, 16 probed).

Coverage: 18 search-lane probes + 12 distinct explain questions (+2 retries), spread across core-infra (consensus, XDR, SCP, horizon, rpc, captive core, friendbot, result codes), SDK/CLI, and named projects (freighter, blend, soroswap, reflector) — bridges/ramps/Etherfuse avoided per brief. STRENGTHS: canonical lane is excellent where mapped — consensus, XDR (3 SDF repos floated), rpc server, soroban rpc, transaction result codes (4 repos), js-sdk signing, SCP nomination, XDR envelope definition all routed correctly with accurate DeepWiki-grounded answers (SCP answer's NominationProtocol/Slot/HerderSCPDriver detail checks out). SYSTEMIC PATTERN: search scoring gives multi-generic-token matches ~10.4 vs flat 5 for exact repo-name matches, so any NL question not caught by the canonical keyword list falls into token-soup top-1; explain inherits that top-1, and since random repos aren't DeepWiki-indexed, 6 of 12 explain questions returned empty answers (answered:false) — an effective ~50% answer rate on deep-code questions, with 4 of the 6 empties caused by mis-routing rather than DeepWiki coverage. The endpoint IS honest about empties (answered:false + note2 + still-cite-repoUrl guidance), so no EMPTY-DISHONEST filed — but note2 telling users the mis-routed repo 'is still the authoritative source' compounds the routing errors. soroswap/core routed correctly but persistently unanswered (DeepWiki coverage gap, honest flags — counted OK). Suggested fix shape: (1) exact/near name-match should dominate NL token scoring (same class as the sls named-entity recall bugs), (2) extend canonical keyword map with stellar-cli, rs-soroban-env/host/rent/ttl, captive-core, friendbot, (3) prefer non-archived repo when a canonical set contains an archived repo plus its live successor.

STALE-SERVED verdict for the cell: largely CLEAN — the classic staleness traps all pass on live probes: wasm target is the current wasm32v1-none (5/5 top results, q=what%20rust%20target...), CLI is post-rename `stellar contract build/init/invoke` everywhere, setup page states CLI v27.0.0 stable, JS SDK v16 upgrade news surfaces for upgrade queries, SEP-24 spec served at current v3.8.0 (updated 2025-09-10), deploy-to-testnet content updated Feb 2026. The cell's real defects are (a) SECTION-LEVEL INGESTION GAPS: dev-docs corpus covers /docs/build/**, /docs/data/** and /meetings/** but not /docs/learn, /docs/tokens, /docs/validators (likely also /docs/networks and /docs/tools — untested), and vector search papers over the holes with tangential SEP-spec/Hubble-schema results at 'high' confidence labels, which will mislead Raven; (b) chunk hygiene (Docusaurus footer+TOC chunks, author-archive pagination dupes) surviving the recent nav-card filter; (c) freshness scoring structurally inert on dev-docs (publishedAt null despite in-prose dates) — the reason staleness is clean today is that the crawl is recent, not that the scorer would catch it. Honesty checks OK: low-recall queries return fewer results than limit (e.g. futurenet → 1 result) rather than padding; no EMPTY-DISHONEST observed. Fully-OK probes (12): deploy-to-testnet, freighter-react, wasm-target, install-cli, friendbot, upgrade-js-sdk, sep-24, getEvents, futurenet, wallet-sdk-mobile (TS/Kotlin/Flutter intro pages), state-archival (top-4), horizon-payment-history (near-perfect top-5).

Coverage caveats + systemic observations: (1) CAPs are NOT in the corpus at all — validSources (from the honest 400 on source=cap) lists sep/dev-docs/sdf-blog/paper/audit/etc. but no CAP source; stellar-protocol /ecosystem (SEPs) is ingested, /core (CAPs) is not. CAP-number queries (e.g. q=CAP-38 AMM) can only be answered tangentially via dev-docs/audits, and generic CAP-governance queries fall through to meetings junk. Not filed as a per-record failure (nothing in-corpus was missed) but it is the biggest coverage gap for a protocol-concepts lane. (2) SEP retrieval itself is strong: for all 9 SEP numbers probed (1,6,7,10,12,24,30,38,41) the authoritative sep source doc ranked top-2, usually with the matching dev-docs page adjacent; source=sep filter works; bogus source returns an honest 400 with the valid list. (3) The dedupe/ordering bug (findings 1-2) is the highest-leverage fix: it contradicts the API's own scoreModel note, hit 3/22 probes, and directly displaced an authoritative sep doc at the requested K. (4) developers.stellar.org/meetings author/pagination pages are a distinct junk doc-class worth an ingest-time filter — they carry high confidence (0.73-0.85) because they're fresh+authoritative even when contentless. (5) Minor: sdf-blog row title 'Protocol 15 Improvements' sits at URL slug /protocol-14-improvements (sponsored-reserves probe) — likely a source-site artifact, not filed.

Coverage: audit source is rich (~40 protocols enumerated across OtterSec/Veridise/certora/Runtime Verification/Halborn/Code4rena/Quarkslab/Coinspect/CoinFabrik/Cantina/OpenZeppelin); known-item '<protocol> audit' recall is strong — 16 OK probes incl. PhoenixDeFiHub, Aquarius (both certora+Cantina), xycLoans, FxDAO, Slender, Spiko, Soroswap core, ClickPesa (0.87 high), Trustless Work, Soroban Governor, Defindex, USTRY-exploit, 'is Blend safe' (incident #1). Systemic patterns: (1) incidents corpus = ONE document, and the highest-value natural phrasings ('stellar hacks', 'what hacks have happened') miss it — incident recall is the cell's weakest axis; (2) short 1-2 word queries degrade sharply: relevance collapses to ~0.15 and the 0.40 confidence floor flattens ranking + silently triggers keyword fallback; (3) dev-docs author-pagination pages (/meetings/authors/*/page/N) are ingested as duplicate documents — a chunk-hygiene class the recent nav-card filter didn't catch; (4) the meta scoreModel note overpromises (best-chunk-per-doc and confidence-order both violated reproducibly). Per no-anchoring rule, bridges/ramps/Etherfuse were not probed except where they surfaced naturally. All probes read-only GETs.

Ground truth established by sampling before grading: corpus DOES contain current content — lumenloop-research through 2026-07-03 (weekly roundup series, USST launch 2026-07-01), sdf-blog through 2026-06-04 (P27 Zipper guide), Q1 2026 quarterly (2026-05-07), P26 Yardstick (2026-05-05); protocol timeline in-corpus P23→P27. So freshness failures here are provable, not opinion. OK probes (6): 'latest stellar news' (limit=5), 'recent stellar announcements', 'Q1 2026 quarterly report execution at network scale' (known-item #1), 'stellar protocol 26 yardstick' (known-item #1), 'stellar roadmap 2026', 'stellar mainnet upgrade vote' (#1 correctly the P27 guide — graded OK on ranking; its broken title filed separately). Systemic patterns: (1) undated dev-docs/SEP chunks are structurally immune to the 0.15 freshness term, so they dominate exactly the queries where freshness matters most; fixing dev-docs publishedAt (dates are in the prose AND the URLs) + demoting author-archive/index pages would clear most of this cell's failures. (2) sdf-blog title extraction regression clusters on late-2025→2026 posts — i.e. the newest content — suggesting a blog template change broke the scraper's title selector; DATA fix, not ranking fix. (3) limit-dependent rescoring means eval results at limit=5 don't predict agent behavior at limit=10. Not covered: scf-proposal source returned 0 on all news-flavored queries (matches map agent's note; didn't probe further as it's out-of-cell). No anchoring on bridges/ramps/Etherfuse — none appeared.

cells.cell: PARTNERS × NON-RAMP lanes (audit firms, wallets, tooling, infrastructure, protocols, legal/agency, filters)
cells.notes: SYSTEMIC: one root cause — the default quality bar requires a non-null tagline, and 19/45 fresh partners (incl. ALL 5 wallets, ALL 4 protocols, reflector, stellarexpert, finclusive) have tagline=null — accounts for 7 of 10 findings. The q-matcher itself is healthy (q=oracle/dex/lending/kyc all retrieve the right record under all=1), so the fix is data backfill (19 taglines) or relaxing the bar, plus adding a filteredOut count to meta.counts for honesty (counts.total=0 currently reads as 'none exist'). WHAT'S GOOD: the audit-firm lane is in excellent shape — type filter, natural queries (smart contract audit, soroban audit, security audit, formal verification, penetration testing) all return exactly the right firms with service-level precision (pentest→halborn only, formal-verification→certora/RV/veridise only). Honest empties verified: type=legal, type=agency, q=hire dev shop, q=custody (no such partners in corpus — correct 0s). type= validates unknown values with an explicit error; accepting=banana coerces to false but honestly echoes it. Archived rows (elroy, wallet-guru) correctly excluded by default, labeled trust=low + excludeFromMatching=true. Not audited here: ramp/anchor q-relevance (other cell); note 7 of the 19 tagline-null hidden records are anchors (clickspesa, coins-ph, honey-coin, fonbnk, yellow-card, bitso, aps-money, finclusive) — the ramp cell should be told the same root cause affects their lane.

Coverage caveats: only 26/114 builders have any location value (88 empty), so all location filtering operates on a 23% slice — location=asia/mena honest-0s reflect data sparsity, not ecosystem reality. No skills field is serialized in builder records; skill= is silently aliased to q (response filters echo shows {'q': 'rust'} when you pass skill=rust), making it a plain substring text-search over name/role/bio/project descriptions — hence the trust/rustic false-positive class. Systemic pattern: the username recall miss + brasil/rio misses are the same 'structured truth must drive inclusion' class as sls-018/019. Leaderboard fully passed: sort=activity/stars/issues each internally consistent (0 order violations across 50/30/20 rows), invalid sort returns 400 with validSorts=[activity,stars,issues], category=Anchor filter precise, ecosystem block asOf 2026-06-30. Nonsense locations (narnia, atlantis, zzzznotaskill) all honest-0 with echoed filters — no EMPTY-DISHONEST behavior found on that axis.

Coverage: 28 probes across all 8 long-tail lanes incl. error-path (bad slug 404; bogus status/quarter/kind/sort/dimension all return 400 + valid-values arrays — uniformly excellent contract honesty). RFP scfRound meta (round 45 Submission / 44 Community Vote / 43 ended, asOf 2026-07-09, source:live) verified EXACT against communityfund.stellar.org/awards ground truth. /api/status freshness honesty is strong: hackathons count:0 carries the curated-vs-DoraHacks disambiguation note, EC stats dated 'as of 2026-06-30', sdfSkills 24h-cache disclosed. /api/analyze is the honesty gold standard (scope + funding-v2 count-basis methodology notes); its hackathon aggregates (97000 USD / 2861 hackers / 12 events) match the list sums exactly. Not filed: stellar-hacks-zk (ended 2026-07-03) shows winners:0 over 345 submissions — meta says pulled live from DoraHacks, judging plausibly pending, not provable stale; /api/skills filtered responses keep the UNfiltered bySource facet (kind=mcp-server returns counts.returned=2 but bySource summing to 30) — low ambiguity nit; leaderboard rank 1 microvault has 1 star but activity sort legitimately rewards its 2026-07-06 commit. Caveats: did not verify DoraHacks upstream numbers (hackersCount/prize) against dorahacks.io; did not probe /api/hackathons/compare (4 lifetime uses). Systemic pattern: the two real data findings are both the known 'omission=negation / dead-linkage' class (hackathon-project linkage unpopulated) plus one stale-precompute (clusters) — the parameter-validation and meta-labeling layers are in excellent shape.

Systemic pattern: cross-lane signposting is absent API-wide — 8 of 12 wrong-lane probes returned confident-looking, on-keyword but off-question results with zero pointer to the lane that actually answers (builders/hackathons/rfps/status/leaderboard/partners). In-lane hints DO exist and prove the pattern is cheap to add (repos meta → deepWikiUrl handoff; projects scfCountBasis → /api/analyze?dimension=funding). The builders endpoint is the gold standard for empty honesty (advisory object: 'filter miss, not an unseeded directory' + external channels) and should be replicated on partners/repos 0-result responses. Enum validation is otherwise excellent: 5/5 primary enums 400-with-valid-list (projects category, research source + missing-q, partners type, analyze dimension); repos language honest-0. matchMode strict/majority/loose-1 labels are an honest weak-match signal that partially mitigates routing misses for attentive agents, but carry no lane pointer. Not misgraded as failures: repos for 'blend protocol audit report' (returned blend contracts + audit tooling — adjacent-useful; certora/OtterSec Blend audits confirmed present in research?source=audit) and repos for 'how to deploy a soroban contract' (relevant repos + deepWiki signpost). Coverage caveats: did not param-probe /api/skills, /api/clusters, /api/changelog; observed in passing (other cells' territory): builders location null on ~81/114 profiles, and /meetings date-titled pages in dev-docs may hide more pagination dupes beyond the author pages.

Coverage: 19 rich-prose records sampled RANDOMLY across all 7 categories (seeded offsets, proportional); ramp/bridge-adjacent records (decaf, yellow-card, squid-router, wowmax) were kept minor per the no-anchoring rule — decaf's finding is about wallet typing, not ramps. 20/31 probes OK: secondary capabilities named in prose DO retrieve well via keyword match (escrow→pakana top-4, prepaid card→amero, virtual cards→chipper #1, tokenized gold→matrixdock #2, trade finance→pipeline, flight-delay/parametric insurance→sentinel #1, money market fund→spiko, verifiable credentials→didstellar, nft marketplace→beamable #3, dex aggregator→wowmax, analytics dashboards→flipside #1). SYSTEMIC PATTERNS: (1) slug-normalization dupe class — all 3 dupe pairs follow name vs name+suffix (liqvid/liqvidXYZ, pakana/pakanaNET, stellar-expert/stellarEXPERT); 3 pairs in a 19-record random sample implies more corpus-wide, and 2 of 3 pairs carry CONTRADICTORY scfAwarded between copies (dupes split the truth) — a targeted same-website/same-github dedup sweep would find the rest (scripts/detect-duplicate-projects.ts exists in the working tree). (2) Secondary-capability failures are NOT mainly missing types[] — prose match compensates — they occur when morphology diverges (multisig/multi-signature, tracker/tracking) or when stemming inverts meaning under authority ranking (custody→non-custodial wallets). (3) types[] under-carries secondary capabilities broadly (beamable NFT-in-prose not typed, retrieval unaffected — not filed as finding), which matters only for facet surfaces today since the API exposes no type= filter.

cells.cell: DATA-TRUTH spot-verify (random Live projects, website-liveness vs served status)
cells.notes: Sampled 18 random Live projects proportionally across 6 categories (7 User-Facing App, 3 Infrastructure, 3 Tooling, 3 Protocol/Contract, 1 Asset, 1 Anchor) via random offsets; no anchoring on bridges/ramps/Etherfuse. Verification = curl HEAD/GET on the row's own links.website with DNS + body-content inspection (parked/squatter pages return 200, so status-code-only checks under-count); repo lastCommitAt used only as corroboration, never sole evidence (stale repo != defunct project, e.g. Stellar Tip's 2020 repo but its Chrome-store listing is live = OK). Systemic pattern: 5/18 (~28%) of randomly sampled Live rows have dead/expired/for-sale/squatted websites — confirms the known 'defunct surfaces as active / no Inactive flag' gap at scale; extrapolated over ~883 Live rows that suggests on the order of 150-250 affected records. The Kunst21 squatted-domain case is a distinct safety sub-class (hijacked URL served to agents), same class as the earlier boss-pay hijacked-URL fix. Clean rows verified by real content, not just 200s: SwiftEx, Flutterwave, Kale, VIA Labs, Hubble, Rumble Fish, Turbolong, USDC, Ping, Cryptoconexion, Expand, PagFinance, Stellar Tip. No status flips made — report only. queriesRun counts distinct probes: 18 record fetches + 18 website liveness checks + 5 reproduction probes; okCount = records verified clean.

confirmed.expected: The D'CENT record (slug='dcent', website dcentwallet.com) top-3 — the query IS the record's own slug and the natural unpunctuated form of its name
confirmed.observed: total:0, no results. Variant probe ?q=dcent%20wallet&limit=50 returns 45 wallet results (lobstr, xbull, freighter...) with dcent absent from ALL of them; only the exact punctuated form ?q=D%27CENT matches (top-1). Apostrophe tokenization makes the record unreachable by its natural name form

verdict.evidence: Re-ran all probes 2026-07-09. (1) https://stellarlight.xyz/api/projects/search?q=dcent&limit=10 → "counts":{"returned":0,"total":0} with advisory "No projects match 'dcent' even after broadening the search" — so semantic broadening also fails. (2) The record exists and its OWN slug is the query string: q=D%27CENT → top-1, {"name":"D'CENT","slug":"dcent","url":"https://stellarlight.xyz/project/dcent","links":{"website":"https://dcentwallet.com/en"},"score":1,"via":"keyword"}. (3) Variant q=dcent%20wallet&limit=50 → 50 wallet results (Lobstr, xBull, Freighter, Hana, Beans...), D'CENT absent from all 50. Only exact punctuated forms match (q=d'cent wallet → rank 1/46). Expectation is fair: the query IS the record's canonical slug (the string in its own /project/dcent URL), so this is ground-truth known-item retrieval, and total=0 means no top-K tolerance question arises. Not correct-but-different behavior: apostrophe tokenization indexing only the punctuated form makes the record unreachable by its natural unpunctuated name and its own slug — a genuine RECALL-MISS. (1) GET /api/projects/search?q=phoenix&limit=6: Coins PH (id 6943362b30bcaa9def8ef208) returned via="semantic" with keys `types`, `prominence`, `verificationLevel` ABSENT from the JSON entirely, plus "logoUrl":null and "anchorProfile":null; raw semantic-hit dump confirms no types/prominence keys. verdict.evidence: CONFIRMED — reproduced and quantified exactly; the claim is if anything understated. (1) Re-ran probe https://stellarlight.xyz/api/projects/search?q=sdk&limit=100: "counts":{"returned":63,"total":63} — the result set is COMPLETE, so absences are exclusions from the match set, not low ranking (top-K tolerance doesn't apply). (2) Ground truth: paginated all 7 categories (?category=X&limit=100&offset=N) over 892 active projects → exactly 141 carry "SDK" in types (matches the claim's 141). 95 of those 141 (67%) are absent from q=sdk's full 63-row set. (3) All four named records verified via their own data: cede types=["SDK"], decentrio ["Indexer","SDK"], nebulavrf ["Gaming","NFT","SDK"], mobula-labs ["Analytics","AI","DEX","SDK"] — all four ABSENT from q=sdk, and none has "sdk" in name/slug/shortDescription. (4) Mechanism proven, not just correlated: ALL 95 absentees lack prose "sdk" (0 counter-examples of a prose-"sdk" record missing), and 14 of the 63 returned are NOT SDK-typed (prose-only matches like walletconnect, klever, hana) — inclusion is purely name/description keyword matching; the types field is not in the match haystack despite matchMode="strict, all keywords matched" and the query token literally equaling the structured type value. (5) No correct-but-different lane: ?type=SDK is silently dropped (filters echo omits it, returns 0/0 — itself EMPTY-DISHONEST-adjacent) and category=SDK is rejected ("Invalid category", valid list has no SDK), so q= is the only path to type-driven retrieval. Expectation is fair per the record's own structured data and the project's own established principle (sls-018/019: structured truth must drive inclusion). Side observation for the fixer: the same record can serialize types inconsistently — q=mobula-labs returned types:null while category browse returned the full 4-value array.

(3) Consequences reproduce: q=nft%20marketplace&limit=10 shows xoxno at rank 4 (types=["RWA"], via strict keyword match on "NFT-based position management"/"marketplace volume" prose) — though note litemint/rarible correctly hold ranks 1-2, so this is a symptom, not a WRONG-TOP. Worse: q=lending%20protocol&limit=20 (60 total) — xoxno ABSENT from top-20 despite its description containing the literal phrase "decentralized lending protocol"; the missing structured type deprives it of inclusion/ranking signal in the exact lane its own record defines (matches the sls-018/019 "structured truth must drive inclusion" class). Correct-but-different explanations checked and rejected: RWA-only cannot be curator intent consistent with the record's prose (RWA is future-tense), and no taxonomy constraint prevents ["Lending","RWA"]. DATA-WRONG label is correct: structured field contradicts the record's own prose, with measurable retrieval fallout in both directions (absent from Lending lane, present in RWA browse pool of 66).

confirmed.observed: only 5/10 Wallet-typed; Warmancer (Gaming,NFT,AI prom=82), DFNS (Security 80), Beamable (Gaming,SDK 80), DeFindex (Lending 78), StellarTerm (DEX 72) outrank Albedo/Rabet/Vesseo, and Freighter (Wallet prom=90) sits at rank 7 below prom-80 non-wallets — adding natural 'on stellar' tokens breaks type dominance

verdict.evidence: CONFIRMED — reproduced 1:1, with one mechanism refinement. (1) Re-ran probe https://stellarlight.xyz/api/projects/search?q=wallets%20on%20stellar&limit=10 → top-10 = Lobstr(Wallet,90), xBull(Wallet,90), Warmancer(Gaming/NFT/AI,82), DFNS(Security,80), Beamable(Gaming/SDK,80), DeFindex(Lending,78), Freighter(Wallet,90 @ rank 7), Hana(Wallet,76), StellarTerm(DEX,72), Beans(Wallet/Payments,70). Exactly 5/10 Wallet-typed; Freighter (SDF official wallet, prominence 90) sits below four prom-78..82 non-wallets including a game. Albedo/Rabet/Vesseo pushed to ranks 11/14/15 (verified via limit=40). All results score=1 via=keyword, so ranking among them is not prominence- or type-driven. (2) Baseline q=wallet&limit=10 → clean 10/10 Wallet-typed (Lobstr, xBull, Freighter, Hana, Beans, Albedo, Rabet, Vesseo, Solar Wallet, Stellar Passport), total=149 — proving the corpus supports a wallet-dominant top-10, so the expectation is fair and this is a provable WRONG-TOP, not ordering taste. (3) MECHANISM CORRECTION: the claim blames the 'on stellar' filler tokens, but isolation shows the trigger is the PLURAL. q=wallets alone → identical diluted top-10, total=140 (same as 'wallets on stellar'); q=wallet%20on%20stellar (singular + filler) → total=149 and the same clean 10/10 Wallet top-10 as plain q=wallet. So 'on'/'stellar' are inert; 'wallets' fails to stem/singularize to 'wallet', losing whatever type/name-field boost drives wallet dominance and falling back to prose-keyword hits (Warmancer's 'tied to your wallet', DeFindex/DFNS/Beamable/StellarTerm prose mentions). Failure mode label WRONG-TOP is correct; area should read 'plural-stemming miss (wallets≠wallet)' rather than 'query-token-dilution/on-stellar'. Impact is the same as claimed: the natural phrasing a Raven user types degrades type dominance while the terse singular works.

Fairness checks passed: (a) projects search has no dedicated seps filter param (filters echo = q/category/scfAwarded/limit/offset only), so q is the only vehicle for this structured field; (b) tolerance irrelevant — mykobo is absent entirely from a 1-result set, not merely low-ranked. The failure GENERALIZES: mykobo is also absent from q=sep-24 (total=8: moneygram/fonbnk/typescript-wallet-sdk/... all prose matchers) and q=sep-6 (total=2: typescript-wallet-sdk, vank) despite its coverage.seps asserting both — coverage.seps drives inclusion for zero SEP queries. Core finding stands: structured coverage.seps does not drive projects-search inclusion (prose-only matching), same class as the sls-018/019 fix.

verdict.evidence: Re-ran both probes 2026-07-09. (1) EDUCATION: https://stellarlight.xyz/api/projects/search?q=education&scfAwarded=true&limit=30 → counts {"returned":9,"total":9}, matchMode "strict", semantic:false. Ground truth built by paging /api/projects/search?scfAwarded=true&limit=100&offset=0..300 (400 SCF projects total): exactly 18 have types containing "Education" — matching the claim's list. Diff: 10 misses = bigger, dapp-world, edunode, haciendo-stellar, onboarding-club, passport, soroban-learn, stellar-global, stellarstrides, the-starship-soroban. Every miss verified from its own record: types includes "Education", scfAwarded:true, and shortDescription contains no "educat" substring (e.g. dapp-world: types ["Education"], desc "Comprehensive learning track on Stellar..."; soroban-learn: types ["Education"], desc "Bringing developers from zero to hero..."). Meanwhile gladius (types WITHOUT Education) IS returned because its prose says "gamifies education" — proving matching is prose-only and `types` does not drive inclusion. Type-recall is actually 8/18 (~44%), slightly worse than the claim's "50%" since 1 of the 9 returned is off-type. (2) GAMING: q=gaming&scfAwarded=true → total=12; ground truth 11 Gaming+SCF projects, overlap only 8; misses exactly venalabs (types ["Education","Gaming"]), stride (["AI","Gaming","NFT","Payments"]), source-of-tales (["Gaming","AI","Security"]) — none with "gam" in prose. (3) Fairness: no correct-but-different path exists — category=education returns {"error":"Invalid category 'education'"} (validCategories is a different taxonomy without Education/Gaming) and there is no types= filter param, so q= is the only lane for this query. Not a tolerance issue: only 9 results returned against limit=15/30, so misses are excluded from the candidate set entirely, not out-ranked. Matches the repo's own documented design principle (sls-018/019: structured truth must drive inclusion). RECALL-MISS label is correct.

confirmed.expected: kes (Asset, Stablecoin — KES is the Kenyan shilling; slug/name are the ISO code) and plausibly honey-coin (coverage.countries=["Kenya"]) in top-5
confirmed.observed: total=1 (changera only). Root cause is systemic: 28 of 32 Asset records have shortDescription=null (ars, brl, pen, kes, ngnc, mxne, tzs, gyen, audd, qcad, …), so natural-language currency queries have nothing to match and the semantic lane didn't fire here (semantic:false). Ticker queries work (q=KES+stablecoin → kes #1) but currency-name queries fail across the board

verdict.evidence: CLAIM CONFIRMED, and stronger than filed. (1) Probe reproduces 3/3: /api/projects/search?q=kenyan+shilling&limit=10 → counts.total=1, only "changera" (its desc literally contains "Kenyan Shillings"), meta.semantic:false; kes absent. (2) The kes record itself: category=Asset, types=["Stablecoin"], name/slug=KES, shortDescription=null, coverage=null, website clickpesa.com; q=KES returns kes at #1 via keyword — so ticker works, currency-name fails. Expectation is fair: KES is the ISO-4217 code for the Kenyan shilling, and a fiat-stablecoin Asset named solely by its ISO code implies the currency-name query (symbols are query-implying fields per the grading discipline); the API itself treats this query class as in-scope (semantic lane fires on peer queries like "japanese yen stablecoin"). (3) Systemic stat verified with one correction: category=Asset&limit=100 → 26 of 32 assets have shortDescription=null (claim said 28; minor overcount, pattern holds): ars, arst, audd, brl, brz, eurckobo, eurs, eurx, gbpx, glousd, gyen, kes, mbrl, mica-rent, mxne, ngnc, pen, qcad, rwf, tzs, usdc, usdx, vchf, veur, xusd, zusd. (4) "Across the board" verified: q=tanzanian+shilling → only clickspesa (tzs absent); q=mexican+peso → only felix-pago (mxne absent); q=argentine+peso → only lemon (ars/arst absent); q=nigerian+naira+stablecoin → 10 results, ngnc absent. The one apparent counter-example, q=japanese+yen+stablecoin → gyen #1, is substring luck ("yen" ⊂ "gyen"), not retrieval working. (5) Stronger than claimed — semantic lane cannot rescue these records either: q=kenyan+shilling+stablecoin fires semantic:true (matchMode loose-1, total=10) and kes is STILL absent while wrong-currency stablecoins fill the top-10 (eurckobo=EUR, glo-dollar/usdc=USD, circle, brale, stables, switchly). A null-description record apparently has nothing to embed, so neither lane can retrieve it. (6) The hedged honey-coin half of the expectation is the weak part and should not be counted: honey-coin's own data contains "Kenya" (it ranks #1 for q=Kenya via coverage.countries+desc) but contains no "shilling", so strict all-keywords correctly excludes it — that is correct-but-different behavior, not a miss. Core finding (kes) survives adversarial review on the record's own data alone.
verdict.correctedMode: RECALL-MISS label is correct for the observed failure, but note the dual class: the root cause is DATA-WRONG (missing field — shortDescription=null on 26/32 Asset records, not 28/32 as filed), which is why both keyword AND semantic lanes fail. Fix is data-side (populate asset descriptions / expand ISO codes to currency names at index time), not ranking-side.

confirmed.expected: ars/arst (ARS-ticker stablecoins) and bitso (coverage.currencies includes "ARS") in top-5; anclap/ping (coverage.countries=["Argentina"]) plausible
confirmed.observed: total=1 (lemon only). Neither asset tickers nor coverage.currencies drive inclusion. Same failure on q=nigerian+naira (total=1, ngnc absent) and q=mexican+peso (total=1, mxne and bitso[currencies MXN] absent). Only q=japanese+yen succeeds — by luck, 'yen' is a substring of the name gYEN

verdict.evidence:
HOLDS as RECALL-MISS, but the claimed mechanism is wrong and must be corrected before filing.

REPRODUCED (2026-07-09): q=argentine+peso → total=1 (lemon only); q=nigerian+naira → total=1 (digitpay-finance, not ngnc); q=mexican+peso → total=1 (felix-pago). All matchMode=strict, semantic=false. Expected records exist with the claimed data: bitso is Live/Anchor with coverage.currencies=["MXN","BRL","ARS","COP","USD"] and coverage.countries=["Mexico","Brazil","Argentina","Colombia"]; ars, arst, mxne, ngnc are Live Stablecoin records (though with EMPTY shortDescription and coverage=null — their only query-implying data is the ticker).

MECHANISM CORRECTION — the claim's sentence "Neither asset tickers nor coverage.currencies drive inclusion" is REFUTED: q=ARS → ars (top) + bitso in top-3; q=MXN → bitso, etherfuse, bando, mxne (total=4); q=NGN → ngnc in top-4 (total=6). Bitso's full record contains "MXN"/"ARS" ONLY inside coverage.currencies (desc says "Mexico", never "MXN"), proving coverage.currencies IS keyword-indexed and drives inclusion. The real defect is (a) missing currency-NAME→ISO-code synonymy ("argentine peso"/"naira"/"mexican peso" never lexically match ARS/NGN/MXN) compounded by (b) strict-AND matching: for q=argentine+peso, "argentine" would match bitso's coverage.countries "Argentina", but "peso" matches nothing in the record, so strict-AND excludes it. q=naira (single word) also misses ngnc (total=2: digitpay-finance, changera — both correct prose matches).

YEN NOTE REFINED: q=japanese+yen → gyen via keyword (substring luck, as claimed) PLUS zusd/xusd/usdc via=semantic — a semantic fallback lane exists and fired here, but did NOT fire for the peso/naira probes (all returned via=keyword only). The inconsistent semantic rescue strengthens the finding. &semantic=true is silently ignored (meta.semantic stays False).

FAIRNESS CHECK PASSED: /api/skills contains no guidance telling agents to query by ISO code (no hits for currency/iso/ticker/alias), so natural-language currency names are in-contract for an agent-facing layer; ISO-4217 name↔code is deterministic, not taste; and this matches the repo's own documented bug classes (strict-AND matching; structured truth must drive inclusion — sls-018/019, #177 query-alias precedent). Strongest single exhibit: bitso on "argentine peso"/"mexican peso" (both query concepts present in its own coverage fields as Argentina+ARS / Mexico+MXN). Weaker but same class: ars/arst/ngnc/mxne, whose records imply the query only via ticker→name mapping (empty descriptions, null coverage — itself a data-thinness observation worth noting in the finding). The records that WERE returned (lemon, digitpay-finance, felix-pago) are all correct results, so this is purely a recall gap, not WRONG-TOP.

confirmed.expected: pen (Asset, Stablecoin; PEN = ISO code for the Peruvian sol, scfAwarded=true) as the on-topic result; certainly not lending/bridge/oracle projects with zero Peru signal in their own data
confirmed.observed: total=113, matchMode=majority — 'sol' substring-matches 'Solana'/'solutions'. Top-5 = blend, allbridge, dia, band, circle (all off-topic per their own records); pen absent entirely. Off-topic substring inflation outranks + drowns a query the corpus can actually answer

verdict.evidence:
OBSERVED HALF REPRODUCES EXACTLY. Re-ran https://stellarlight.xyz/api/projects/search?q=peruvian+sol&limit=5 -> total=113, matchMode=majority, top-5 = blend, allbridge, dia, band, circle. Paged through ALL 113 (offset 0-113): pen absent entirely. Isolation probes prove substring inflation: q=peruvian -> total=0 (nothing in corpus matches it), q=sol -> total=113 with the IDENTICAL top-5 in strict mode, so every hit comes from 'sol' as a 3-letter substring (Solana in bridge descriptions, solar-wallet, sollpay, solang, bousol, 'solutions'). Worse, these off-topic hits are served with confidence.score=0.97, label='high', relevance:1 — per-result confidence contradicts the meta's own majority-mode disclosure. That defect is real.

EXPECTED HALF REFUTED — the WRONG-TOP label fails the ground-truth rule. pen's OWN record contains no data implying this query: shortDescription=null, coverage=null, no currency/symbol metadata; the live page https://stellarlight.xyz/project/pen literally renders 'About PEN — No description available.' The PEN->Peruvian sol link is external ISO knowledge, not the record's data. Corpus-wide Peru signal is zero: projects q=peruvian -> 0; q=peru -> 1 (perun, a bridge — 'Peru' substring of 'Perun', itself another substring-inflation instance); partners q=peru -> 0 (even Anclap's partner record has no Peru coverage). So 'a query the corpus can actually answer' is false — pen was never retrievable from its own data, and no on-topic record was outranked. WRONG-TOP's premise (on-topic loser exists) does not hold.

WHAT ACTUALLY HOLDS: (1) DATA-WRONG on pen — an SCF R43-awarded Asset/Stablecoin (anclap.com) whose record never states which currency it's a stablecoin for; that missing field is the root cause the query is unanswerable. (2) The 'sol' substring inflation serving 113 off-topic results at relevance:1/'high' confidence is a genuine, independently reproducible ranking/honesty defect (probe: q=sol vs q=solana total=128), but it should be filed against the confidence/matching contract, not as this WRONG-TOP.
verdict.correctedMode: DATA-WRONG (pen record missing currency/description/coverage — root cause; the substring-inflation confidence defect is real but is a separate finding, not WRONG-TOP since no on-topic record was outranked)

confirmed.observed: two records, same website https://cashabroad.one/: cash-abroad (category=Anchor, types=[Payments,Anchor], scfAwarded=TRUE, coverage.countries=[Mexico]) and cashabroad (category=User-Facing App, types=[Payments], scfAwarded=FALSE, coverage=null). verdict.evidence: CONFIRMED, reproduced 3x. (1) Original probe https://stellarlight.xyz/api/projects/search?q=cashabroad&limit=5 returns both records exactly as claimed: `cash-abroad` (name "Cash Abroad", category=Anchor, types=[Payments,Anchor], scfAwarded=true, scfTotalAwardedUSD=180025, scfAwardedRounds=[11,14,21], verificationLevel="Verified (Community)", coverage.countries=[Mexico], website=https://cashabroad.one/) and `cashabroad` (name "CashAbroad", category=User-Facing App, types=[Payments], scfAwarded=false, scfAwardedRounds=[], verificationLevel="Unverified", coverage=null, website=https://cashabroad.one/ — its shortDescription is raw grant-application prose: "We request a $104,000 grant..."). Same website, same company. (2) Refutation attempt "maybe scfAwarded is per-submission so both could be right" fails by the API's own documented semantics: /api/analyze?dimension=funding countBasis states "Counts distinct PROJECTS with scf.awarded=true (not awarded submissions)" — scfAwarded is a project-level fact, so the same project carrying both true and false is a provable contradiction from the corpus's own data. (3) Refutation attempt "maybe they're intentionally distinct entities" fails: both have canonicalSlug=null, lifecycle=null, status=Live — no field marks either as an alias/variant, and the anchor entity is already separately represented in the partners lane (cash-abroad.anchorProfile → /partners/anchor-cash-abroad), so two project rows is pure duplication. (4) Impact is WORSE than claimed: probe ?q=cashabroad&scfAwarded=true (meta confirms scfAwardedOnly:true was parsed) still returns the scfAwarded=false twin (and zusd, also false) — so even SCF-filtered consumers see the phantom; this also hints at a separate filter-not-filtering issue outside this claim's scope. (5) Both twins appear at ranks 1-2 on the natural query ?q=cashabroad%20payments%20mexico&limit=10, well within any tolerance. Label DATA-WRONG (duplicates/scfAwarded) is correct.

confirmed.expected: either projects with scfAwarded=false only, or an explicit signpost that scfAwarded accepts only 'true' (awarded-only mode)
confirmed.observed: scfAwarded=false is silently treated as no-filter: meta.filters echoes scfAwarded:false yet #1 result is warmancer with scfAwarded=true (total=22 = unfiltered gaming). An agent asking 'gaming projects that did NOT get SCF funding' gets SCF winners with no warning

verdict.evidence: Re-ran all three probes 2026-07-09. (1) https://stellarlight.xyz/api/projects/search?q=gaming&scfAwarded=false&limit=15 → meta.filters echoes {"q":"gaming","scfAwarded":false,"scfAwardedOnly":false}, counts {"returned":15,"total":22}, #1 result warmancer with "scfAwarded":true,"scfTotalAwardedUSD":136500 (also beamable true at #2). (2) Same query with NO scfAwarded param → identical: total=22, identical slug list, and meta.filters ALSO echoes "scfAwarded":false — proving false is the internal default and the param is a pure no-op, while the echo misleads callers into thinking their filter was applied. (3) scfAwarded=true → total=12, every result scfAwarded:true, so the filter machinery exists but only in the awarded-only direction. Fairness checks: the gaming set contains 10 scfAwarded=false projects (kale, katagames, cyberbrawl, open-gamefi-sdk, kalien, cryptocannoneer, stellar-quest, ...), so a working false-filter would return a distinct non-empty set; and the live OpenAPI spec (https://stellarlight.xyz/api/openapi.json, /api/projects/search) declares scfAwarded as {"type":"boolean"} with description "Filter to SCF-funded projects only" — boolean type invites passing false, and nothing documents that false is ignored. No signpost/warning anywhere in the response. Claim CONFIRMED: scfAwarded=false is silently treated as no-filter while being echoed in meta.filters; an agent asking for non-SCF-funded gaming projects receives SCF winners ranked first with no warning. EMPTY-DISHONEST is the right label per the taxonomy's own example ("silently ignored param"), noting the response is non-empty — the dishonesty is the applied-looking echo, not a 0-result.

verdict.evidence: Probe reproduces byte-for-byte: GET https://stellarlight.xyz/api/projects/search?q=moneygram&limit=1 returns coverage={"countries":[],"currencies":[],"seps":["sep-24"],"asOf":"2026-07-07"} while the same record's shortDescription says "users deposit and withdraw cash to and from USDC at ~500K retail locations across 170+ cash-out countries". Attempted refutation via spec semantics FAILED: the live OpenAPI spec (https://stellarlight.xyz/api/openapi.json, Project.coverage) states the field "answers 'which anchors serve currency/corridor X?' with filterable, dated fields instead of prose... Synced from the matching partner record; null for non-anchors or when no partner record matches" — i.e. null is the documented unknown signal, so a non-null object with fresh asOf ("cite as the as-of date") positively asserts zero countries/currencies. Attempted refutation via data-unavailability FAILED: the sync SOURCE has the data in-corpus — /api/partners?q=anclap shows assets:["PEN","ARS"] yet anclap coverage.currencies=[]; moneygram's partner record has regions:["global"] yet coverage.countries=[]; bitso proves the pipeline can fill both (countries=['Mexico','Brazil','Argentina','Colombia'], currencies=['MXN','BRL','ARS','COP','USD']). Scale re-measured: of 19 category=Anchor projects, 10 carry non-null coverage and 7/10 have currencies=[] (moneygram, honey-coin, cash-abroad, anclap, trace-finance, ping, clickspesa); moneygram uniquely has countries=[] too. Bonus: honey-coin countries=['Kenya'] vs its own prose "18+ markets (Kenya, Nigeria, Ghana, Tanzania, Uganda, etc.)" — partially defensible under the spec's "Primary market" wording. Minor correction: claim said 12 records carry coverage; live count within category=Anchor is 10 (immaterial). Expectation is fair — the claim accepted null=unknown, which is exactly the spec's own contract. DATA-WRONG label correct (ambiguous-null/omission=negation class on the field whose stated purpose is filterable corridor answers).

verdict.evidence: Re-ran all probes 2026-07-09; claim reproduces exactly and survives adversarial checks. (1) https://stellarlight.xyz/api/projects/search?q=is%20keybase%20still%20maintained&limit=5 → projects:[] with "matchMode":"majority","semantic":false,"counts":{"returned":0,"total":0}; advisory says "No projects match 'is keybase still maintained'... better answered against the research corpus" and suggests /api/research — a misdirect for a known-item lookup. (2) Baseline q=keybase → 1/1, Keybase top-1, "status":"Inactive" — the record exists and its name token is literally in the failing query, so the expectation is maximally fair (known-item recall, top-5 trivially generous for a 1-record match). (3) q=keybase%20still%20active → 0 results, semantic=false (reproduces). (4) Inconsistency confirmed and strengthened: I ran the IDENTICAL template on both names — q=is%20orbitcdp%20still%20active → semantic:true, returns [orbit-finance, orbitcdp] via:"semantic"; q=is%20keybase%20still%20active (same shape, same words) → 0 results, semantic:false. So the failure is not word-choice ("maintained" vs "active") — the semantic fallback rescues one project but never fires (semantic:false) for the other on the same query shape. (5) Checked for correct-but-different mitigation: the response's codeReferences side-channel does include keybase/client with project.slug="keybase", but it carries no status field (so it cannot answer the lifecycle question — and its lastCommitAt 2026-07-02 would actively suggest "maintained" while the project record says Inactive), and the advisory simultaneously asserts no projects match — contradicting the side-channel. Primary contract (projects array) fails known-item recall. RECALL-MISS label is correct; mechanism is majority-of-4-tokens requiring filler words ("is","still","maintained") to match, with an unreliable semantic rescue.

confirmed.expected: raum-network in top-10 — the query IS the record's own canonical slug (the identifier served in every API row and /project/ URL); q=raum and q=raum%20network both return it top-1

verdict.evidence: Re-ran all probes 2026-07-09 and the claim reproduces exactly, with a sharpened mechanism. (1) q=raum-network&limit=10 → 3 results, all via:"semantic": expand-network, baf-nework, blockeden — raum-network absent. Yet the record exists with that exact canonical slug: q=raum → raum-network top-1 via:"keyword" (score 1), and q=raum%20network → raum-network sole result. (2) q=spectra-finance → 4 semantic results (trace-finance, ripe-money, chaincred, orbit-finance), spectra-finance absent; q=spectra → spectra-finance top-1 via:"keyword". Expectation fairness confirmed: the slug is the identifier the API itself serves in every row and in the /project/ URL — a known-item query by the system's own canonical ID failing top-10 is a provable recall defect, not taste (same class as sls-018/019). Not correct-but-different behavior: search handles the partial and spaced forms of the same name, so hyphen form failing is inconsistency, not design. Mechanism refinement: the keyword lane fails on ALL hyphenated queries, not intermittently — tested 6 hyphenated slugs (raum-network, spectra-finance, ultra-swap, orbit-finance, fx-swap, trace-finance, ripe-money) and every one returned via:"semantic" only (keyword lane = 0 hits), while un-hyphenated prefixes hit via:"keyword". The claimed 'intermittency' is actually the semantic fallback: it rescues the record itself in 5 cases (ultra-swap, orbit-finance, fx-swap, trace-finance, ripe-money all self-retrieve top-1 via semantic) but not for raum-network/spectra-finance. Aggravator: the wrong results are served with misleading confidence — expand-network on q=raum-network carries confidence 0.97 label:"high" relevance:1.
verdict.correctedMode: RECALL-MISS (label correct; note the underlying keyword-lane hyphen tokenization failure is deterministic across all hyphenated queries — only the semantic rescue is intermittent)

confirmed.expected: Temporal browse intent ('newest projects', 'recently launched') either answered recency-ordered or signposted to the in-corpus temporal lane — /api/leaderboard?sort=activity exists and carries lastActivityAt per project — since the directory has no launch/added-date ordering
confirmed.observed: 36 arbitrary keyword-coincidence matches (soroban-optimistic-oracle, pg-atlas, venalabs...) served at score=1, confidence 0.97/label 'high', relevance 1, with NO advisory and no signpost; q=recently%20launched likewise returns moneygram/ondo/redstone-finance as if they answer the question. /api/changelog is contract-changes-only; analyze has no recency dimension. An agent would confidently present arbitrary projects as 'the newest'

verdict.evidence:
Re-ran all probes 2026-07-09 ~17:47Z; claim reproduces fully and survives fairness checks.

1) PROBE REPRODUCES: GET /api/projects/search?q=newest%20projects&limit=5 → counts.total=36, top-5 = soroban-optimistic-oracle, pg-atlas, venalabs, stellarstrides, soroban-security-portal — ALL at score=1, via="keyword", confidence {"score":0.97,"label":"high","relevance":1,"freshness":1}. No advisory key in meta or top level (meta keys: counts, filters, generatedAt, matchMode, matchModeLabel, scfCountBasis, semantic, source). Results are NOT recency-ordered: rank-1 SOO has lastActivityAt=2025-04-13, while the actually-most-recent projects (Microvault/Noir/Pipeline, lastActivityAt=2026-07-06 per leaderboard) are absent from the top-5.

2) q=recently%20launched&limit=5 → total=6: moneygram (prominence 90, project lastActivityAt=2022-06-24), ondo, redstone-finance at confidence 0.92 "high". These match the WORD "launched" in prose and are prominence-ordered, not launch-date-ordered — a genuinely new project whose prose lacks "launched" is unfindable via this lane, so the intent is answered by coincidence, not by data.

3) ALTERNATIVE LANE EXISTS: /api/leaderboard?sort=activity&limit=5 returns projects rank-ordered by github.lastActivityAt (rank 1 Microvault 2026-07-06T11:55:55Z) — an in-corpus temporal lane. /api/changelog meta.note confirms "Curated feed of contract-affecting changes" (not project recency). /api/analyze validDimensions=["all","hackathons","categories","funding"] — no recency dimension. So leaderboard?sort=activity is the only temporal signpost target, and none is given.

4) KEY FAIRNESS CHECK — the advisory mechanism EXISTS and fires elsewhere, so its absence here is an omission, not a missing feature: /api/projects/search with no q returns error:"no_query" plus advisory{summary, suggestions:[{action:"retry-with-q",...}]}; q=zxqvbn%20flurble (0 results) returns an advisory suggesting alternate lanes ("...thesis-level question that's better ans[wered by]..."). Temporal-browse intent gets neither an advisory nor honest confidence — it gets relevance=1/confidence 0.97 "high", the exact shape that tells a consuming agent (Raven) "these ARE the newest projects."

5) LABEL CHECK: ROUTING-MISS is the right mode. Not EMPTY-DISHONEST (response isn't empty/error); not WRONG-TOP (no on-topic record exists in this lane to be outranked — the lane has no launch/added-date field to order by). The query's intent is approximately answerable by leaderboard?sort=activity (lastActivityAt), and the existing advisory pattern is the natural signpost vehicle. One honest caveat for the fixer: sort=activity is "most recently active," not strictly "newest/launched" — no lane has launch-date ordering, so the correct fix is a signposting advisory (possibly plus lowered confidence when intent-words like newest/latest/recently are consumed as content keywords), not a claim that leaderboard fully answers it.

confirmed.observed: Param silently dropped: returns Live wallets (lobstr/xbull/freighter...), meta.filters echo omits status entirely. 'Which projects are inactive?' is unanswerable with no warning

verdict.evidence:
CONFIRMED — reproduced and every premise verified; refutation attempts failed.

1) Probe reproduces exactly. /api/projects/search?q=wallet&status=Inactive&limit=5 returns 5 Live wallets (Lobstr, xBull, Freighter, Hana, Beans), counts.total=149 — byte-identical total to the same query WITHOUT status, proving the param had zero effect. meta.filters echo = {"q":"wallet","category":null,"scfAwarded":false,"scfAwardedOnly":false,"limit":5,"offset":0} — status absent, no warning/advisory anywhere in meta.

2) Distribution premise verified EXACTLY via Payload where-counts: Live=883, Development=5, Inactive=3 (Keybase, OrbitCDP, Orbit Finance), Pre-Release=1. The status enum is real, populated, and documented in the OpenAPI Project schema ("'Inactive' = defunct/archived (e.g. product shut down) — such projects stay name-searchable but are heavily down-ranked and excluded from the leaderboard/directory"), so lifecycle queries are implied by the data's own contract, not auditor taste.

3) No alternate spelling works: lifecycle=Inactive, projectStatus=Inactive, inactive=true, status=inactive all ignored identically (same filters echo, Live results). /api/analyze?dimension=lifecycle and =status both return {"error":"unknown dimension", validDimensions:["all","hackathons","categories","funding"]}. Secondary probe confirmed: q=shut%20down → 3 Live security apps (ChainPatrol, Cushion, Stellarscam.report); OrbitCDP absent even though its own lifecycle.note reads "Shut down in 2026 — team pivoted to Zenex" — lifecycle.note is not indexed.

4) Fairness of expecting a warning: (a) sibling documented lanes DO accept status= (/api/hackathons?status=upcoming, /api/rfps?status=open per the published skill doc), so an agent generalizing the family convention hits this silently; (b) the same endpoint already ships an advisory mechanism for exactly this class — its no_query response says "If the term was sent under a different field name, note this endpoint keys off `q`" — so warning on dropped params is the established house pattern.

5) Failed refutations / one scope nuance: status is NOT a documented request param in the OpenAPI spec (params: q, category, scfAwarded, limit, offset), so this is silently-ignored-undocumented-param (the literal EMPTY-DISHONEST definition), not spec drift. And the raw Payload REST lane technically CAN enumerate (/api/projects?where[status][equals]=Inactive returns exactly the 3), but that lane appears in neither /api/status endpoints[], the skill doc, nor the MCP tools — so "no lane" holds for the advertised agent-facing surface; state it as "no documented lane".
verdict.correctedMode: EMPTY-DISHONEST (correct as labeled; secondary ROUTING-MISS flavor — the undocumented Payload REST lane can answer but nothing signposts it)

With relevance flat, ordering falls to prominence/authority (lobstr prominence 90, vesseo 78, klever 60 vs equitx/investar/stocken 0), so the rare discriminative token 'stocks' (3 corpus matches, the query's topic noun; equitx shortDescription: "synthetic assets mirroring the value of real-world stocks", types RWA; investar: "Dhaka Stock Exchange (DSE) equities or US securities"; stocken types RWA) is weighted identically to the generic verb 'buy' (14 matches, boilerplate in wallet descriptions like "buy, swap, send"). This is not taste-based reordering among equally-valid results — 9 records with no stocks connection outrank all on-topic ones. (1) Probe re-run: https://stellarlight.xyz/api/projects/search?q=save+money+in+dollars&limit=25 → matchMode:"loose-1", semantic:false, counts.total=8; results = vesseo, bridge, surgepay, piggy-wallet, digibank-sdp, figo, tago-cash, changera. sava and dollarize are absent from the ENTIRE candidate set (total=8), not merely outside top-5, so no top-K tolerance quibble. The corpus's own semantic lane certifies them as THE dollar-savings answers: q=dollar+savings+account → matchMode:"strict", semantic:true, returns exactly sava (via keyword, score 3) + dollarize (via semantic, score 0.726), total=2. (3) Mechanism isolated by controlled swap: q=savings+money+in+dollars (same intent, 'save'→'savings') → sava appears at rank 5 (loose-1, total=9); q=save+dollars → sava rank 7. So plural folding works ('dollars'→'dollar') but 'save' does not match 'savings' (no stemming); in the natural phrasing sava matches only 1 of 3 tokens and fails the loose-1 (all-but-one) bar. Because 8 loose-1 keyword hits exist, the semantic fallback that would retrieve both records is suppressed (semantic:false). (4) Not correct-but-different behavior: the returned set is token noise, e.g. bridge — a B2B stablecoin-infrastructure company — ranks #2 for a consumer savings-intent query while both products whose own data says "savings account"/"dollar account" are excluded. RECALL-MISS label is correct.

(2) ?q=largest%20dex → total=39 and the major DEXs ARE included at ranks 2-6 (conf 0.65), proving the system's own intended behavior for unmatched superlative tokens is include-at-reduced-confidence; (3) ?q=dex%20volume → total=41, majors at ranks 6-10. Adding the no-op token "largest" collapses 41→2 and excludes every prominent DEX at any K (total=2 < top-10 tolerance). Not correct-but-different: the relaxed path exists but doesn't fire because 2 hits > 0 blocks semantic fallback, and the two wrong survivors are served at high confidence with relevance:1 — actively misleading to a consuming agent (would answer "largest Stellar DEX = Spinach"). RECALL-MISS label is correct (strict-AND-on-prose bug class; structured types=DEX should drive inclusion). Minor claim nit: WOWMAX conf is 0.92 not 0.97 — immaterial.

verdict.evidence: Re-ran probe https://stellarlight.xyz/api/projects/search?q=blend%20vs%20yieldblox&limit=6 — reproduces exactly: semantic=true, matchMode=strict, counts total=3, results = Yieldblox (via keyword, conf 0.92 high), Blox Global (semantic, 0.90), Backyard (semantic, 0.94). Blend absent from the ENTIRE result set, not just top-3, so no top-K tolerance defense. Expectation verified fair: baseline ?q=blend&limit=5 returns Blend #1 (prominence 90, conf 0.97, 17 total) — the exact name token in the query implies retrieval from the record's own data. Ruled out correct-but-different behavior: reversed-order probe ?q=yieldblox%20vs%20blend DOES return Blend (#4 via semantic, 0.94), proving asymmetric/inconsistent handling rather than a deliberate no-comparison-queries policy; ?q=blend%20yieldblox (no 'vs') also drops Blend (total=1, Yieldblox only). Mechanism: strict-AND keyword lane — Yieldblox's shortDescription mentions 'Blend' so it matches both name tokens, while Blend's record never mentions 'yieldblox' and is excluded; semantic backfill fails to recover it in this token order while confidently serving fuzzy-name noise (Backyard 0.94 high). Matches the project's documented strict-AND bug class. RECALL-MISS label is correct.

verdict.evidence: Re-ran https://stellarlight.xyz/api/projects/search?q=most%20used%20wallet&limit=10 — reproduces exactly: #1 Sava (types=[], category "User-Facing App", Turkish savings app, prominence 0, score=2, conf 0.97/high), #2 SocketFi (types=[SDK,Payments,Security], score=2, conf 0.97/high); Lobstr/xBull/Freighter (types=["Wallet"], prominence 90) demoted to #3-5 with score=1, conf 0.65/0.65/0.60 medium. Baseline q=wallet confirms the expectation is fair: Lobstr/xBull/Freighter rank top-3 at conf 0.97/0.97/0.92 high — so the confidence signal is inverted purely by adding superlative tokens. Mechanism verified adversarially: Sava's prose contains "most" ("Most stop at the exchange") and "wallet" but NOT "used", yet matchMode reports "strict / all keywords matched" (a secondary contract lie); and q=most%20popular%20wallet returns byte-identical results (same total=47, same top-5), proving unmatched tokens are silently dropped while the stopword "most" is scored as a content keyword — prose hits on "most"+"wallet" (score 2) beat Wallet-typed prominence-90 leaders (score 1), and Sava's confidence.relevance=1.0 on a wallet query contradicts its own types=[]. Mitigations checked and insufficient to refute: true wallets remain within top-5 (so not RECALL-MISS), and SocketFi self-describes as a "smart wallet dApp" (making #2 borderline), but #1 Sava is provably off-topic by its own structured data — WRONG-TOP label is correct as filed. Additional sub-finding beyond the claim: the false "all keywords matched" matchMode label borders on EMPTY-DISHONEST for the ignored-token behavior.

verdict.evidence: Re-ran probe https://stellarlight.xyz/api/projects/search?q=largest%20stablecoin&limit=10 (2026-07-09): reproduces exactly. #1 yellow-card (types:['Anchor'], score 2, confidence 0.92/high, relevance 1) — its prose "Africa's largest licensed stablecoin fiat on-ramp" supplies the spurious 'largest' match; #2 bridge (types:['Payments'], score 2, confidence 0.97/high, relevance 1) — its 'largest ever' refers to "Stripe's ~$1.1B acquisition of Bridge — its largest ever"; #3 circle (types:['Stablecoin'], prominence 90) demoted to 0.6/medium relevance 0.5; #7 usdc (types:['Stablecoin']) at 0.65/medium relevance 0.5. Baseline q=stablecoin confirms Circle #1 at 0.92/high, relevance 1. Decisive refutation-attempt that failed: synonym probe q=biggest%20stablecoin returns Circle #1 (0.92/high, rel 1), Glo Dollar #2, USDC #5 — 'biggest' appears in no prose so ranking is sane; 'largest' flips the top-2 to wrong-type records solely because the token incidentally appears in their descriptions modifying 'on-ramp'/'acquisition'. Fairness checks: (a) Bridge does issue MGUSD so it's stablecoin-adjacent, but its own types=['Payments'] and the matched 'largest' is about the Stripe acquisition; Yellow Card's own types=['Anchor'] — neither is a stablecoin by its own structured data; (b) not tie-ordering taste: the confidence contract is inverted (relevance=1/high for wrong-type records vs 0.5/medium for type-matched stablecoins), which would steer a downstream agent to answer 'Yellow Card' for 'largest stablecoin'. Mechanism: strict-AND keyword scoring (matchMode:strict, score=2 for 2/2 tokens) treats the superlative qualifier as a topical keyword and derives confidence.relevance from match fraction, so incidental prose containing 'largest' outranks types=Stablecoin + prominence 90. WRONG-TOP label is correct.

confirmed.expected: Blend/Soroswap/Aquarius (types Lending/DEX, prom 90/90/85, status Live) — the records the corpus itself marks as the prominent live DeFi protocols

verdict.evidence: Re-ran https://stellarlight.xyz/api/projects/search?q=most%20active%20defi%20protocol&limit=10 three times — stable each run: total=10 (not 7), semantic=true, order = Sava, BIM Exchange, Backyard, BIM, Sushi, Rumble Fish, TEACHMEDEFI (all via:keyword, prom=0), then Blend (via:semantic, score 0.685, conf 0.95 high), ZKLiquid, Orbit Finance (status Inactive). So the claim's observed evidence is wrong on both counts: Blend IS in top-10 at #8, and total=10. RECALL-MISS under the stated top-10 tolerance is refuted for Blend (claimant likely hit a transient semantic-lane failure — their 7 rows equal today's 7 keyword rows exactly). However the corrected finding holds as WRONG-TOP: positions 1-7 are prominence=0 records, several off-type for the query (Sava category User-Facing App, types=[] — its prose contains 'Most stop at the exchange' and 'not a DeFi dashboard', which is what loose-1 keyword matching latched onto; TEACHMEDEFI types=['Education']; BIM/BIM Exchange types=['Bridge']), outranking the corpus's own prominent DeFi records. Baseline probe https://stellarlight.xyz/api/projects/search?q=defi&limit=10 (matchMode strict, total=72) returns Reflector/Blend/Aquarius (prom 90/90/85, Live, Lending/DEX) as top-3 — the engine's own ground truth for 'defi'. Yet Aquarius (DEX, prom 85) and Soroswap (DEX, prom 90, Live; fields confirmed via ?q=soroswap) are entirely absent from the superlative query's top-10 because the ranking-directive tokens 'most'/'active' pollute the keyword lane, and keyword rows always outrank semantic rows. Prom-0/type-mismatched rows at #1-#7 above prom-90 on-type records is provable from record data, not ordering taste. Minor note: Orbit Finance served at #10 with status Inactive but confidence label 'high' (0.83).
verdict.correctedMode: WRONG-TOP (not RECALL-MISS): Blend is present at #8 via the semantic lane, within the top-10 tolerance, so recall of the flagship answer succeeds; the real defect is prom-0, off-type keyword matches (Sava, TEACHMEDEFI, BIM x2) occupying #1-#7 above the corpus's prominent DeFi protocols, with Aquarius/Soroswap (DEX, prom 85/90) pushed out of top-10 entirely.

confirmed.observed: Sushi (prom 0) at 0.92/high, Sava (savings app, types=[]) and Backyard at 0.97/high occupy #1-3; the prom-90/85/80 DEXes demoted to #4-6 at conf 0.65/medium

verdict.evidence: Re-ran probe https://stellarlight.xyz/api/projects/search?q=most%20popular%20dex&limit=8 (2026-07-09T17:50Z): reproduces exactly — 1. Sushi (types=['DEX'] prom=0 score=2 conf=0.92/high rel=1 auth=0.6), 2. Sava (types=[] prom=0 score=2 conf=0.97/high), 3. Backyard (types=[] prom=0 score=2 conf=0.97/high), then Soroswap/Aquarius/Phoenix (types=['DEX'] prom=90/85/80 score=1 conf=0.65/medium rel=0.5) at #4-6. Baseline q=dex serves Soroswap/Aquarius/Phoenix 1-2-3 at 0.97/high, Sushi outside top-8. Isolation probes: q=popular%20dex → total=157 (identical to plain dex, 'popular' silently dropped) and CORRECT ranking; q=most%20dex → total=40 with the same inverted Sushi/Sava/Backyard top-3. Root cause: the superlative filler 'most' is scored as a content keyword — none of the top-3 shortDescriptions contain 'popular' or literal 'dex', but all three contain 'most' (Sushi: 'One of the most established DeFi protocols'; Sava: 'Most stop at the exchange'), giving them score=2 vs 1 and rel=1 vs 0.5. Sava (category User-Facing App, a Turkish lira savings app) and Backyard (stablecoin yield strategies), both types=[], are provably non-DEX by their own structured data yet outrank three prom-90/85/80 typed DEXes AND carry higher confidence (0.97/high vs 0.65/medium) — the confidence signal inverts, so an agent trusting top-1 or labels gets a savings app as 'most popular dex'. Refutation attempts failed: not consistent-by-design ('popular' is stopworded but 'most' is not), not a tolerance/taste issue (WRONG-TOP = off-topic outranking on-topic, satisfied by #2-3). Minor refinement: Sushi at #1 is the weakest element (it is types=['DEX'] and globally a popular DEX), but its rank derives from the 'most' token (prom=0, absent from plain-dex top-8); Sava/Backyard make the finding airtight without it.

verdict.evidence: Re-ran probe https://stellarlight.xyz/api/repos/search?q=programmable+smart+wallet&limit=5 (2026-07-09T17:51Z): rank-1 row reproduces exactly as claimed — {"fullName":"sorobanhooks/freighter","url":"https://github.com/sorobanhooks/aptopia-wallet","isFork":true,"stars":0,"project":null,"repoScore":48,"codeDepth":0.7189,"judgeScore":0.8,"judgedHackathon":"Agentic Hackathon","deepWikiUrl":"https://deepwiki.com/sorobanhooks/freighter"}. So the served fullName is stale/false vs GitHub's current state while url is current — the row contradicts itself, provable from its own two fields. Stellar NFT repos rank 14-17: #14 snnac ('Stellar NFT NFC ACcess'), #15 litemint/litemint-pfp-contract ('NFT smart contract... on Stellar', scfAwarded=true, repoScore=15), #16 findolor/sorodrop ('Stellar airdrops... token & NFT', 2024-09), #17 besmetaverse-indexer ('indexer for NFT marketplaces') — all score=3 desc-only matches vs score=5 name/topic matches. Refutation attempts failed: (a) GitHub check of dappradar/nft-sales-adapters src/adapters shows 25 adapters, zero Stellar/Soroban — no Stellar relevance despite DappRadar being a curated directory project; (b) expected records' superiority is provable from their own data — octoplace (#10, repoScore=3, empty desc, dead Solidity) outranks litemint-pfp (#15, scfAwarded, repoScore=15, self-described Stellar NFT contract) purely on name-token score 5 vs 3; (c) mechanism confirmed: all score=5 rows match 'nft' in name/topics, score=3-4 rows in description only, no chain-relevance factor; (d) violation is inside top-10 tolerance and the on-topic rows fall outside top-10 entirely. Minor claim imprecision: dappradar repo is multi-chain (not 'Ethereum') and its parent project is Stellar-linked, but the repo contains no Stellar adapter. Same class as known project_repo_search_relevance_defect, here text-field-weighting-driven rather than authority-driven.
